LAW AND MOTION 302, SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S, LONDON, BOSTON MARKS INSURANCE BROKERS, LTD, BOSTON MARKS INSURANCE BROKERS, LLC, CRAWFORD & COMPANY, OSNIEL GARCIA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ESTABLISH FACTUAL BASES FOR THIS COURT TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANTS. (SEE BBA AVIATION PLC V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 190 CAL.APP.4TH 421, 428-29.) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR A CONTINUANCE FOR JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DISCOVERY IS LIKELY TO LEAD TO FACTS ESTABLISHING JURISDICTION. (SEE IN RE AUTO ANTITRUST CASES I AND II (2005) 135 CAL.APP.4TH 100, 126-27.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Cory A. Birnberg, Esq. (415-398-1040) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Suzanne N. McNulty of Fitzpatrick & Hunt et al LLP (213-873-2120) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Specially Appearing DEFENDANTS CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S, LONDON, BOSTON MARKS INSURANCE BROKERS, LTD, BOSTON MARKS INSURANCE BROKERS, LLC, CRAWFORD & COMPANY, OSNIEL GARCIA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to establish factual bases for this Court to exercise jurisdiction over defendants. (See BBA Aviation PLC v. Superior Court (2010) 190 Cal.App.4th 421, 428-29.) Plaintiff's motion for a continuance for jurisdictional discovery is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate that discovery is likely to lead to facts establishing jurisdiction. (See In re Auto Antitrust Cases I and II (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 100, 126-27.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.
